sections.paragraph:
εὐωχέω (s. ἔχω and Boisacq 300, also next entry; Hdt. et al.; Philo; Joseph.; pap; mid. since Alcaeus, Fgm. 70, 5 L-P.; Jdth 1:16; 3 Macc 6:40; pap; εὐωχηθείς OGI 168, 11) in Gk. lit. the act. of ἐ. refers to provision of sumptuous entertainment (Hdt. et al.); the mid. εὐωχεῖσθαι to feast (Alcaeus, above, et al.) is also used, as AcPl Ha 7, 10 (prob. in ref. to an Agape celebration after the Eucharist, s. Schubart p. 51, n. 10; in ref. to cultic festivities IGLSyria 1, 157f [I b.c.]; s. HDörrie, GGAbh 60, ’64, 81).

Boisacq Boisacq = ÉB., Dictionnaire étymologique de la Langue Greque—List 6

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Alcaeus Alcaeus , lyric poet, VII–VI b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

L-P. L-P. = ELobel/DPage; s. AnthLG

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Gk. Gk. = Greek

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

act. act. = active

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

prob. prob. = probable, probably

ref. ref. = reference(s)

IGLSyria IGLSyria = Inscriptions grecques et latines de la Syrie—List 3

GGAbh GGAbh = Abhandlungen der Gesellschaft der Wissenschaften zu Göttingen—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 417
